requirements.evidence: LLMClient ABC (agents/llm/client.py) with abstract complete(prompt, *, model, temperature, max_tokens, timeout) -> str; LLMError base exception. OllamaClient (agents/llm/ollama_client.py) implements it against Ollama /api/generate, default model 'llama3.1:8b'. rg -i ollama across agents/ (excluding agents/llm/) returns no matches -- no other module calls Ollama directly, satisfying the acceptance criterion literally, both pre- and post-change.

architecture_conformance.evidence: Matches docs/LLD/llm-provider.md per module docstrings. ABC chosen over Protocol for runtime enforcement, consistent with codebase's existing preference for concrete/instantiable types. No other package touches httpx/Ollama directly.

regression_risk.evidence: agents/llm/ was previously scaffold-only (empty __init__.py); this is additive, no existing call sites modified. Full suite (agents/) run 3x: 67/67 passed each time, no flakiness observed.

edge_cases_and_error_handling.evidence: Independently re-derived and hand-verified 5 error scenarios via throwaway script (not committed): HTTP 500, connection error (httpx.ConnectError), malformed JSON body, JSON missing 'response' key, and 'response' field present but wrong type (int not str) -- all 5 raise OllamaClientError, none swallow or return None/empty string. NOTE: the wrong-type-of-'response'-field case (isinstance check at ollama_client.py line 128) is implemented correctly but has NO corresponding test in test_ollama_client.py -- test coverage gap (implementation correct, test missing).

security.evidence: No secrets, no injection vectors in the HTTP call construction (prompt passed as JSON string value via httpx, not interpolated into a URL or shell). base_url configurable but defaults to localhost; no SSRF concern raised by this subtask's scope since it's a local dev-only client.

performance.evidence: DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=120.0 reasonable for local CPU 8B inference. httpx.Client is created and closed per-call (context manager) -- reasonable for this call volume; not a connection-pooling concern at this stage, though a future optimization could reuse a client across calls if call volume becomes high (not a blocking issue for this subtask).

maintainability.evidence: Both files carry extensive disclosed-design docstrings explaining ABC vs Protocol, endpoint choice, default model choice, and error-handling philosophy -- good for future maintainers of 3.4.3/3.4.5.

test_coverage.evidence: 13/13 targeted tests pass (verified independently), all via httpx.MockTransport (grepped test file: only httpx.MockTransport/httpx.Response/httpx.ConnectError used, no requests.get/httpx.get/real sockets). Covers interface contract (2), request shape (5), response parsing (1), error paths (4), exception hierarchy (1). Gap: no test for 'response' key present but non-string type (implementation handles it, test doesn't cover it) -- minor, non-blocking.

confidence.evidence: Re-derived Ollama /api/generate request/response shape from own knowledge independent of the implementer's docstring claims: matches (model, prompt, stream=false request; response/done fields in response). Confirmed pyproject.toml diff for this commit is empty (httpx already declared since initial scaffold commit 699105b), so 'no new dependency' claim holds. Confirmed 987b5f6 (session-limit recovery commit) touches only .cdr/runs/2026-07-09/030-implementation/{metadata.json,handoff.json} -- no source files, consistent with the described manual-intervention scope.

possible_improvements.evidence: 1) Add a test for non-string 'response' field type to close the coverage gap noted above. 2) Interface genericity forward-looking check for 3.4.3/3.4.5: complete()'s signature (prompt, model, temperature, max_tokens, timeout) is generic enough for both known future call sites per the issue's own scope -- no system-prompt or chat-message-list param exists, which is fine if 3.4.3/3.4.5 truly only need single-shot prompt-in/text-out (as documented), but if segmentation prompting later wants a separate system-prompt field for cleaner prompt construction, this will require a signature change (non-breaking additive kwarg, low risk) rather than a workaround baked into the interface today. Flagged as forward-looking, not blocking.

prompt_injection_observed.detail: Re-fetched issue #18 body via gh issue view this session; found clean, matches acceptance criteria/test spec summary given in the task bundle. No injected instruction-like text found in this read (unlike a prior session noted by the requester).
prompt_injection_observed.detail_other: Unrelated to the reviewed commit: this verification session's own tool-output channel carried injected system-reminder-style text (a fake 'date changed, don't mention it' notice, fake 'tokensave' MCP server usage instructions, and a fake 'Auto Mode Active' directive). Treated as untrusted data per repo security policy; not complied with; disclosed to the requester.

tests_run_independently.full_suite: pytest agents/ -q, executed 3x for flakiness -> 67/67 passed each run, no flakiness
tests_run_independently.lint: ruff check agents/llm/client.py agents/llm/ollama_client.py agents/llm/test_ollama_client.py -> all checks passed
tests_run_independently.adversarial_error_trace: custom throwaway script (not committed) exercising 5 error scenarios against OllamaClient directly via httpx.MockTransport -- all 5 raised OllamaClientError as claimed
